title: Lane 4 route-feel runway
summary: Run a parallel route-feel wave focused on support readability, route-local replay variants, notebook filing depth, and authoring guardrails without station-shell or geometry dependencies.

execution_order.goal: Scope one route-support readability pass using existing inspect/chip/seam behavior.
execution_order.why_now: The support slot exists; players should feel its difference without more station text.

execution_order.goal: Implement the smallest support-readability improvement with route/controller coverage.
execution_order.why_now: Route-feel can improve independently from station shell and content packs.

execution_order.goal: Review support readability for clarity, no HUD drift, and stable support behavior.
execution_order.why_now: Support should feel legible without growing into loadout UI.

execution_order.goal: Scope route-local replay variants tied to existing world-state, process, or nursery seams.
execution_order.why_now: Soft replay can make outings feel alive without a separate replay system.

execution_order.goal: Implement one or two route-local replay variants through existing route fields and tests.
execution_order.why_now: The route catalog boundary is now ready for better authored variants.

execution_order.goal: Review route replay variants for canonical filing stability and no hidden progression drift.
execution_order.why_now: Replay flavor must not change saved route identity.

execution_order.goal: Scope notebook filing depth and route authoring guardrails in the new catalog boundary.
execution_order.why_now: More route content is coming; authors need sharper guardrails before breadth grows.

execution_order.goal: Implement one filing-depth or catalog-guardrail pass with focused route tests.
execution_order.why_now: Better route authoring support reduces future integration costs.

execution_order.goal: Review the route-feel runway and decide whether lane 4 is ready for a new chapter-grade outing.
execution_order.why_now: Lane 4 should end this wave with cleaner route semantics and less coordinator risk.

completion_gate: Support readability, replay variants, and filing/catalog guardrails each land as route-owned improvements or are explicitly scoped down by scout/critic.
Canonical filed route titles and saved identities remain stable.
No station shell restructure, geometry expansion, broad content pack, planner, loadout, save schema, combat, or economy lands.
Focused route/controller/station/snapshot/runtime tests and build pass after runtime changes.
guardrails: Lane 4 owns route semantics and Route v2 feel.
Keep support choice tiny and route-facing.
Use existing world-state, process, nursery, and notebook filing seams.
Do not add a fourth Source to Shore beat in this packet.
Do not absorb lane 1 station shell, lane 2 content pack, or lane 3 geometry work.

scout_contract.finding: Support behavior is already split correctly across hand-lens inspect retargeting, note-tabs hint bias, place-tab question framing, and route-marker map targets. The weak readable moment is the generic OUTING SUPPORT notice shown when the player cycles the tiny support row.
scout_contract.implementation_choice: Make the existing support-switch notice contextual from the current active route/controller state. Do not add a support row, planner, loadout, station layout, save field, route catalog shape, or support mechanic.
seams: src/engine/field-request-controller.ts owns getOutingSupportNoticeText(...), getFieldRequestHintState(...), inspect retargeting, and support-biased hint projection.
src/engine/field-requests.ts owns ActiveFieldRequest, progressLabel, target biome, routeV2 status, selectedSupportId, and active process/world-state route titles.
src/engine/field-request-state.ts owns route-marker location and ready-to-file map-calm activeOuting suppression.
src/engine/game.ts owns toggleOutingSupport() and already calls the controller and notice helper from the station support row.
src/engine/field-season-wrap.ts already provides station-side support-aware TODAY copy and should stay out of this pass unless a focused regression proves otherwise.
main_484_scope: Extend getOutingSupportNoticeText(...) so it can use selected support plus ActiveFieldRequest | null.
Keep generic fallback support notice copy compact for no-active-route states.

For active gathering Route v2 states, make route-marker name the target biome/location, hand-lens name current route clues, note-tabs name current compact progress or route aim, and place-tab tie the place question to the current route title.
For ready-to-synthesize states, use calm ready-note copy that does not imply a map marker or in-field hunt.
Update toggleOutingSupport() to pass the current active request from getFieldRequestController() after the selected support id changes.
test_contract: src/test/field-request-controller.test.ts: generic fallback copy, active-route contextual support notice copy for all four support ids, ready-to-file no-marker/no-hunt support notice copy, and existing support split behavior.
src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts: one station support-row toggle on an active route shows contextual OUTING SUPPORT notice and route-marker still focuses the world-map target.
src/test/field-requests.test.ts only if implementation needs an extra ready-to-file active-request assertion.
out_of_scope: No broad content pack, geometry change, station shell restructuring, planner UI, save-schema change, route framework, support id, inventory/loadout row, route id/evidence id/slot-order change, or Source to Shore fourth beat.
Do not move or reshape src/engine/field-request-catalog.ts.
Do not change filed route identity or ready-to-file map-calm behavior.

implemented_shape: Extended getOutingSupportNoticeText(...) in src/engine/field-request-controller.ts so the existing support-switch notice can use selected support plus ActiveFieldRequest | null.
Updated toggleOutingSupport() in src/engine/game.ts to pass the current controller active request after the selected support id changes.

Kept hand-lens retargeting, note-tabs hint bias, place-tab station wrap behavior, route-marker map target resolution, route catalog shape, save schema, route ids, evidence ids, and ready-to-file map-calm behavior unchanged.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/field-request-controller.test.ts -t "support notice|Source to Shore support|place-tab|route-marker"
npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "support row|route marker"
npm test -- --run src/test/field-request-controller.test.ts src/test/field-requests.test.ts
npm test -- --run src/test/field-season-board.test.ts src/test/save-snapshots.test.ts -t "Source to Shore|High Pass|route-marker|support|field station"
npm run build
main_484_implementation.handoff: Promote ECO-20260428-critic-484 to review support readability for tiny route-facing copy, unchanged support semantics, and ready-to-file map-calm behavior.

confirmed: Support readability stays inside the existing OUTING SUPPORT notice.
Ready-to-file routes use calm ready-note copy and do not imply marker, replay, or in-field hunt behavior.
Support row size, station UI, route catalog shape, save schema, route ids, evidence ids, ordered slots, Source to Shore beat count, and filed route identity remain stable.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/field-request-controller.test.ts -t "support notice|Source to Shore support|place-tab|route-marker"
npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "support row|route marker"
critic_484_review.handoff: Promote ECO-20260428-scout-485 to scope route-local replay variants through existing route/world-state/process/nursery seams.

scout_485_contract.finding: Source to Shore already has active-only variants for Source Shelter and Forest Release. Dune Catch is the missing third-beat active variant, and Coastal Scrub already has the sand-capture process seam needed to drive it.
selected_scope: Add one active-only Dune Catch variant named Held Dune.
Trigger it only while active source-to-shore-dune-catch overlaps the existing Coastal Scrub sand-capture process moment.
Keep ready-to-file and filed Dune Catch canonical.
implementation_contract: Add processFocus to source-to-shore-dune-catch with momentId sand-capture, activeTitle Held Dune, and short active summary copy.
Mirror the same active-only title and Source to Shore board/map/atlas copy in resolveDuneCatchState(...) when phase is active and sand-capture is live.
Do not add Source to Shore to getReplayNote(...) unless a focused test proves the current SourceToShoreState seam cannot carry the variant.

Do not change SOURCE_TO_SHORE_BEAT_FLOW, route ids, evidence ids, ordered slots, support behavior, ready-to-file map calmness, filed notebook identity, save schema, station shell, content, or geometry.
test_contract: src/test/field-requests.test.ts: add Dune Catch to the live-only variant matrix and prove ready/filed canonical identity.
src/test/field-season-board.test.ts: extend Source to Shore active variant coverage so Dune Catch surfaces Held Dune only during the sand-capture window.
src/test/save-snapshots.test.ts: update only if an existing Source to Shore snapshot needs active title coverage.
src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts: add or extend one focused Dune Catch active surface smoke only if board/unit coverage is insufficient.
out_of_scope: No new replay system, planner, save schema, station shell, route framework, content pack, geometry, support behavior, route id, evidence id, slot-order change, filed-title change, or fourth Source to Shore beat.
Do not broaden Source to Shore beyond the existing Source Shelter -> Forest Release -> Dune Catch endpoint.

implemented_shape: Added processFocus to source-to-shore-dune-catch through the existing Coastal Scrub sand-capture process moment.
Mirrored the active-only Held Dune title/copy through SourceToShoreState so station, atlas, active outing, and world-map surfaces stay aligned.

Kept ready-to-file and filed Dune Catch canonical, with no Source to Shore beat-count, route id, evidence id, slot-order, support behavior, save schema, station shell, content, geometry, or replay-framework change.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/field-requests.test.ts -t "route variants|Dune Catch|Source to Shore"
npm test -- --run src/test/field-season-board.test.ts -t "Source to Shore|Dune Catch|Held Dune"
npm test -- --run src/test/save-snapshots.test.ts -t "Source to Shore|Dune Catch"
npm test -- --run src/test/field-request-controller.test.ts -t "Source to Shore|Dune Catch|route-marker|support notice"
npm run build
main_485_implementation.handoff: Promote ECO-20260428-critic-485 to review active-only Held Dune behavior, canonical ready/filed Dune Catch identity, and lane boundaries.

confirmed: Held Dune is active-only and tied to the existing Coastal Scrub sand-capture process seam.
Ready-to-file and filed Dune Catch identity, evidence, and notebook filing behavior remain canonical.
Source to Shore remains a three-beat route with unchanged route ids, evidence ids, ordered slots, support behavior, save schema, station shell, content, geometry, and replay-framework boundaries.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/field-requests.test.ts -t "route variants|Dune Catch|Source to Shore"
npm test -- --run src/test/field-season-board.test.ts -t "Source to Shore|Dune Catch|Held Dune"
npm test -- --run src/test/save-snapshots.test.ts -t "Source to Shore|Dune Catch"
critic_485_review.handoff: Promote ECO-20260428-scout-486 to scope notebook filing depth and route catalog guardrails.

scout_486_contract.finding: Route behavior and filing synthesis are stable; the next lane-4 risk is future catalog authoring drift around canonical filed text, slot ordering, clue-backed filing tails, and active alternate clue references.
selected_scope: Add a focused catalog-authoring guard test over FIELD_REQUEST_DEFINITIONS.
Document the filedText/displayPrefix/active-alternate boundary in content authoring guidance.
Keep the implementation test/docs-only unless a failing guard proves a tiny helper is needed.
implementation_contract: Assert route ids are unique, routeV2Note.readyTitle stays NOTEBOOK READY, and ready copy names the canonical route title.
For evidence routes, assert evidence slot ids are unique, slotOrder exactly matches evidence-slot ids when present, and clue-backed filed synthesis routes keep a clueBackedTail.
Assert processFocus/worldStateFocus activeTitle labels do not leak into routeV2Note.filedText; displayPrefix can remain display-only and must not rewrite canonical filed text.
Assert activeSlotEntryIdsBySlotId keys target real evidence slots and alternate entry ids exist in the route biome, without requiring alternates to be base slot entries.
Do not change route runtime behavior, save shape, support behavior, route ids, evidence ids, ordered slots, station shell, Source to Shore beat count, or filed route identity.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/field-request-catalog.test.ts
npm test -- --run src/test/field-requests.test.ts -t "full-arc filed-note synthesis matrix|route variants"
npm run build
If runtime code is touched unexpectedly, also run focused route/controller/station/snapshot slices from this packet.

implemented_shape: Added a focused route catalog authoring guard test over FIELD_REQUEST_DEFINITIONS.
Guarded unique route ids, NOTEBOOK READY copy, evidence slot uniqueness, exact slotOrder coverage, clue-backed filed-note tails, display-only replay labels, and active alternate clue references.
Documented the filedText/displayPrefix/active-alternate boundary in docs/content-authoring.md.
Kept the pass test/docs-only with no route runtime, save, support, station, content, geometry, route framework, or Source to Shore beat-count change.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/field-request-catalog.test.ts
npm test -- --run src/test/field-requests.test.ts -t "full-arc filed-note synthesis matrix|route variants"
npm run build
main_486_implementation.handoff: Promote ECO-20260428-critic-486 to review the catalog guardrail and decide whether packet 179 can close.

confirmed: The catalog guardrail is test/docs-only and directly protects Route v2 authoring boundaries.
Canonical filed text, display-only replay prefixes, exact evidence-slot ordering, clue-backed filing tails, and active alternate clue references are now covered.

Route ids, evidence ids, ordered slots, support behavior, filed states, save schema, station shell, content, geometry, route framework, support system, planner, and Source to Shore beat count remain unchanged.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/field-request-catalog.test.ts
npm test -- --run src/test/field-requests.test.ts -t "full-arc filed-note synthesis matrix|route variants"
npm run build
critic_486_review.handoff: Packet 179 is done; lane 4 has no remaining actionable work in this runway.

post_packet_path: If clean, lane 4 can propose the next chapter-grade outing.
If route work needs new content or geometry, write handoffs instead of blocking the lane.
